notes: All entities listed as alleged unless noted. Trial began Nov 2025 before Tribunal Oral Federal No. 7. 87 defendants: 22 ex-officials, 65 businessmen. 175 documented bribe payments. Notebooks written by Oscar Centeno, driver of Roberto Baratta.
provenance_warning: EVIDENCE ANOMALY (audited 2026-03-24): (1) Chain of custody BROKEN — originals lost 18+ months, Notebook #5 permanently missing, originals resurfaced from unidentified source 4 days before 2019 elections. (2) Gendarmería pericia (Aug 2025, 312 pages) confirmed 1,500+ alterations by 2-4 non-Centeno hands. (3) Jorge Bacigalupo charged (Nov 2025) with falsifying names — specifically crossing out real names to implicate other individuals. (4) Centeno refused to testify at trial (March 19, 2026). (5) 25 arrepentido confessions obtained under detention-release mechanism challenged as coercive. (6) CNDC cartelization investigation archived Sept 2023 finding no conclusive evidence. Independent corroboration varies by entity — see verification_status on individual nodes.

companies.name: Electroingeniería S.A.
companies.notes: Executives Gerardo Ferreyra and Jorge Guillermo Neira detained. Neira became cooperating witness.

companies.name: Austral Construcciones S.A.
companies.notes: Owned by Lázaro Báez. Also subject to separate money laundering trial.

companies.name: CPC S.A.
companies.notes: Linked to Cristóbal López and Fabián De Sousa. Attributed 5 bribe payments totaling approx. 14 million pesos (2011-2012).

companies.name: Esuco S.A.
companies.notes: Carlos Wagner, president of Cámara Argentina de la Construcción, was its director. Cooperating witness. Alleged ~1.2 billion pesos in contract values linked to bribes.

companies.name: JCR S.A.
companies.notes: Owned by Juan Carlos Relats. Attributed 40 of the 175 bribe payments, totaling 85,493,691 pesos — the highest single-company total.

companies.name: Iecsa S.A.
companies.notes: Angelo Calcaterra (cousin of Mauricio Macri) became cooperating witness. Héctor Javier Sánchez Caballero also cooperated.

companies.name: Grupo Roggio (Benito Roggio e Hijos S.A.)
companies.notes: Aldo Roggio and Claudio Glazman became cooperating witnesses.

companies.name: Helport S.A.
companies.notes: Mentioned by cooperating witnesses as regular bribe payer.

companies.name: Cartellone Construcciones Civiles S.A.
companies.notes: Listed among companies making deliveries to officials.

companies.name: Decavial S.A.
companies.notes: Representative Aznar mentioned in bribe delivery testimony.

companies.name: COARCO S.A.
companies.notes: Representative Gerbi mentioned in bribe delivery testimony.

companies.name: Isolux Corsán Argentina
companies.notes: Juan Carlos de Goycoechea became cooperating witness.

companies.name: Techint (Organización Techint)
companies.notes: Héctor Alberto Zabaleta became cooperating witness.

intermediaries.name: Ernesto Clarens
intermediaries.notes: Described as the financial operator of the bribe system. Testified he collected approx. US$300,000 per week on orders from Néstor Kirchner. Estimated total handled: ~US$30 million.

intermediaries.name: Oscar Centeno
intermediaries.notes: Driver of Roberto Baratta. Author of the notebooks that originated the case. Cooperating witness.

intermediaries.name: Daniel Muñoz
intermediaries.notes: Deceased former private secretary of Néstor Kirchner. Alleged recipient of bribe deliveries between Jan 2008 and Sep 2010.

intermediaries.name: Claudio Uberti
intermediaries.notes: Former head of OCCOVI (Órgano de Control de Concesiones Viales). Cooperating witness.

projects.name: Obra pública vial (cartelización)
projects.notes: Systematic cartelization of road public works. Companies agreed on bid allocations; ~20% overpricing with half allegedly destined for bribes. Organized through Cámara Argentina de la Construcción under Carlos Wagner.

politicians.name: Cristina Fernández de Kirchner
politicians.notes: Tribunal attributed 175 bribe payments to her totaling 171,224,710 pesos and US$613,000. Trial in progress.

politicians.name: Julio De Vido
politicians.notes: Accused as organizer of the illicit association.

politicians.name: Roberto Baratta
politicians.position: Subsecretario de Coordinación, Ministerio de Planificación
politicians.notes: Accused as organizer. His secretary was accused of collecting 68 bribe payments.

politicians.name: José Francisco López
politicians.notes: Cooperating witness. Previously detained with bags of cash at a monastery.

politicians.name: Ricardo Jaime
politicians.notes: Accused as organizer of the illicit association.

politicians.name: Carlos Santiago Kirchner
politicians.notes: Accused as member of the illicit association.
